pattern: Sorting jobs by difficulty and tracking the best profit available at each difficulty lets each worker, taken in ability order, greedily claim the most profitable job they can do.
hints: A worker should take whichever doable job pays most, and a harder job is never worth less than an easier one once you carry the running best.
Pair each job's difficulty with its profit and sort by difficulty; sort workers by ability so you can sweep both together.
Walk workers from least to most able, advancing a pointer over the sorted jobs while difficulty is within reach and keeping the maximum profit seen, then add that maximum for each worker.

edge_inputs.description: first example, several workers reach mid-tier jobs
second example, no worker can do any job
single job and single worker who can do it
an easier job pays more than a harder one
all workers below the easiest difficulty
every worker can do the top job

stress.description: random jobs and workers across the value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    m = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    diff = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    prof = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    work = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [diff, prof, work]
stress.note: independent random arrays stress the greedy sweep and pointer

complexity.time: O(n log n + m log m)
